join_order.notes: Use customer_segment_postings to enumerate only BUILDING customers, then traverse orders_cust_postings per customer and apply o_orderdate immediately so only qualifying orders are materialized.

join_order.build: orders(filtered by segment and o_orderdate, range-partitioned by o_orderkey)
join_order.probe: lineitem(via contiguous slices of lineitem_order_postings per orderkey partition)
join_order.notes: Partition qualifying orders into cache-sized orderkey ranges, then process each partition against the matching contiguous offsets, rowids, and payload spans from lineitem_order_postings to avoid cache-miss-heavy random probes.

scan.table: customer(filtered via postings) -> orders(postings expansion) -> lineitem(cache-sized orderkey partitions)
scan.access_pattern: random on customer and orders postings during selective expansion, then partition-local contiguous access on lineitem_order_postings offsets, rowids, and payload columns

data_structures.fact_join: compact_hash_map(pre_sized per orderkey partition) over qualifying orders + pre_built_index(orders_cust_postings,lineitem_order_postings)
data_structures.aggregation: sorted_group_by with register accumulator per qualifying order and fixed_min_heap(10) per thread

indexes.purpose: enumerate BUILDING customer rowids without scanning customer
expand qualifying customers to orders without scanning all 15M orders
read contiguous lineitem slices for each orderkey partition instead of random per-order probes across the full postings structure

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category B physical implementation mismatch. The surviving plan already removed most logical waste, but its hot path still performs about 1.46M probes into the cache-exceeding lineitem_order_postings structures (~480 MB offsets plus ~480 MB rowids) and then fetches lineitem payloads with poor locality. Replace the full orders scan and global order sort with a selective postings-driven pipeline: enumerate BUILDING customers from customer_segment_postings, expand through orders_cust_postings, apply o_orderdate immediately, and partition the ~1.46M surviving orders by orderkey range so each partition's offsets, rowids, and payload slice stays within LLC. Process partitions independently with thread-local top-k merge, avoiding the dense 15M-key state from iteration 3 and the expensive global sort/materialization from iteration 4.
